title: A Merry Madrigal.
content:
Brightly dawns our wedding day;
    Joyous hour, we give thee greeting!
    Whither, whither art thou fleeting?
    Fickle moment, prithee stay!
    What though mortal joys be hollow?
    Pleasures come, if sorrows follow:
    Though the tocsin sound, ere long,
    Ding dong! Ding dong!
    Yet until the shadows fall
    Over one and over all,
    Sing a merry madrigal 
     Fal la!

    Let us dry the ready tear;
    Though the hours are surely creeping,
    Little need for woeful weeping,
    Till the sad sundown is near.
    All must sip the cup of sorrow 
    I to-day and thou to-morrow:
    This the close of every song 
    Ding dong! Ding dong!
    What, though solemn shadows fall,
    Sooner, later, over all?
    Sing a merry madrigal 
     Fal la!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Schwenck Gilbert, titled "A Merry Madrigal.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
